name: Shamshir-e Zomorrodnegar
description: The Shamshir-e Zomorrodnegar, or Emerald-Studded Sword, is a legendary weapon from Persian mythology, primarily known from the epic poem Amir Arsalan-e Rumi. This sword is no ordinary blade; it possesses immense power and is often associated with malevolent forces. It was crafted by a powerful sorcerer and is adorned with numerous emeralds, each contributing to its magical potency. The sword's most notable feature is its vulnerability: it can only be destroyed by striking it with another specific weapon, the club of Shahpour, a legendary hero. The Shamshir-e Zomorrodnegar is often wielded by formidable villains, posing a significant threat to heroes in Persian folktales. Its emeralds are not merely decorative; they amplify the wielder's strength and magical abilities, making them nearly invincible. The sword represents ultimate power and the constant struggle between good and evil, a recurring theme in Persian mythology. Obtaining and wielding this sword often comes at a great cost, corrupting the wielder and leading them down a path of darkness. Its existence serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked power and the importance of moral integrity. The sword’s legend persists, reminding audiences of the enduring themes within Persian storytelling.
subtitle: The Emerald-Studded Sword of Legend
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly hidden in a magical realm.

keyMyths.title: The Forging of the Sword
keyMyths.description: The Shamshir-e Zomorrodnegar was created by a powerful sorcerer, imbued with dark magic and adorned with emeralds to amplify its power. Its creation marked the beginning of a reign of terror.

keyMyths.title: The Weakness of the Sword
keyMyths.description: Despite its immense power, the sword possesses a single vulnerability: it can only be destroyed by the club of Shahpour. This weakness is a key element in the story of Amir Arsalan.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: Primary source for the legend of the Shamshir-e Zomorrodnegar.
sources.text: Primary source for the legend of the Shamshir-e Zomorrodnegar.
